testcases.input: s = "LeetCode@LeetCode.com" Output: "l*****e@leetcode.com" Explanation: s is an email address. The name and domain are converted to lowercase, and the middle of the name is replaced by 5 asterisks.
testcases.expectedOutput: "l*****e@leetcode.com" Explanation: s is an email address. The name and domain are converted to lowercase, and the middle of the name is replaced by 5 asterisks.

testcases.input: s = "AB@qq.com" Output: "a*****b@qq.com" Explanation: s is an email address. The name and domain are converted to lowercase, and the middle of the name is replaced by 5 asterisks. Note that even though "ab" is 2 characters, it still must have 5 asterisks in the middle.
testcases.expectedOutput: "a*****b@qq.com" Explanation: s is an email address. The name and domain are converted to lowercase, and the middle of the name is replaced by 5 asterisks. Note that even though "ab" is 2 characters, it still must have 5 asterisks in the middle.

testcases.input: s = "1(234)567-890" Output: "***-***-7890" Explanation: s is a phone number. There are 10 digits, so the local number is 10 digits and the country code is 0 digits. Thus, the resulting masked number is "***-***-7890".
testcases.expectedOutput: "***-***-7890" Explanation: s is a phone number. There are 10 digits, so the local number is 10 digits and the country code is 0 digits. Thus, the resulting masked number is "***-***-7890".
